column8: MECHANICAL EFFECT
Mechanical Effect (full description)

Upon spawning or increasing merge rank, Alchemist brews an elixir for 30 seconds, then throws it at 2 units increasing attack speed by 20% for 20s. Each elixir increases all Alchemists' attack speed by 0.25%, and after 50 elixirs they get a single buff of 30%.

Upon spawning or increasing merge rank, Alchemist brews an elixir for 30 seconds then throws it at an enemy unit, stunning them for 6 seconds (PvP). Every elixir brewed increases the damage done by all Alchemists by 0.3%, and after 50 elixirs they get a single buff of 40%.
When they merge or boost their merge rank, Alchemists instantly brew and throw an elixir.
The Alchemist has a 30% chance of using 2 elixirs.

If the Alchemist's trap failed to do any damage by the time it disappears, there's a 75% chance that it will generate an Explosive Slug that detonates upon contact with its first target to do 300% of the trap's damage to the area.

When the Alchemist is merged or his merge rank is increased, summons a Golem that blocks the monsters' movement and deals periodic damage equal to 750% of the trap's damage for 5.8 sec. The damage dealt is increased by 25% for each of the Alchemist's traps on the path. After defending the player, the Golem becomes a mini boss on the opponent's path (PvP).

The Alchemist has a 20% chance of appearing with a Philosopher's Stone that increases his crit chance by 5%. After merging or increasing merge rank, 3 other Alchemists start brewing a new elixir. The Alchemist himself also starts brewing a new elixir immediately after creating the previous one.

Every attack by the Archer has a 15% chance of hitting 2 random targets with a special Poison Arrow, which will periodically inflict the venom for 170% of the Archer's damage and increase the venomous infection damage received by 20%. Each hit from a Poison Arrow will increase the periodic damage by 10%, up to a maximum of 1600%.
Every attack by the Archer has a 15% chance of hitting a random target with a special Exploding Arrow. The Exploding Arrow inflicts 350% of the Archer's damage as area damage.
When an Archer is summoned or merged, all Archers will inflict 30% extra damage with special arrows for 20s.
When an Archer is summoned or merged, all Archers on the player's field will release 1 extra special arrows for 20s. These extra arrows will inflict 70% less damage.

When an Archer is summoned or merged, and also with a 5% chance when a monster dies, a random Archer will summon a hawk that moves towards the monsters and inflicts 400% of the Archer's attack damage. Every hawk receives a hunting mark, which increases the hawk's damage by 10% for each mark.

Whenever a boss or mini boss appears, every Archer will launch a battle hawk at it. The hawk will inflict 600% of the Archer's damage in the area affected, and will also place a hunting mark on monsters in area. Every mark increases the Archer's damage against that target by 5%.

There's a 15% chance of a Ranger appearing in place of the Archer, with a hawk on his shoulder. The Ranger has a bonus 2.5% chance of inflicting critical damage and a 30% chance of firing special arrows. Jagged Tip or Robust Bowstring are always active, and any merging or spawning of an Archer will increase their bonuses by 2 times.
Banshee damage is increased by 18% for every Banshee in the chain. Maximum damage is reached with 6 Banshees.
If there are no more than 4 Banshees on the field, Banshee attack radius is increased by 30% and damage by 35%.
Using an active ability has a 10% chance to increase the merge rank of a random Banshee. The chance increases by 5% for every Banshee on the field.
If there are 1 or fewer Banshees on the field, merging units results in a new Banshee. Damage of all Banshees is increased by 10%.
Movement speed of monsters within the Banshee's attack radius is reduced by 30%. Armor of monsters within the Banshee's attack radius is reduced by 15%.
Monsters within the Banshees' radius die at 25% health, while bosses die at 20% health.
The Banshee's critical attack chance is increased by 5%. The duration of bosses and mini bosses being unable to use their abilities during the Shriek increases to 4 seconds.

Bards at the edges of the field become Drummers. Drummers gain Inspiration only from monsters in the area around them. In both modes they do not spend mana, but their mana accumulation rate is greatly reduced. In the Music mode, gaining inspiration is greatly reduced, but they consume mana. Also, the armor of monsters in the area is reduced by 2% per Drummer's merge rank.

If there are no Accordionists on the field, one will appear at the next merging. The maximum number of Accordionists will increase at Inspiration levels 75, 150 and 300. The damage dealt by the Accordionist increases by 2% per rank; the bonus increases in proportion to the number of regular Bards with lutes, but does not exceed 42%.
After receiving Inspiration level 1, a random Bard afflicted with a negative effect has a 7.5% chance to be cleansed of it.
In the Music mode, the Bard receives 300% bonus Inspiration if a boss is on the field, or 200% bonus Inspiration if there is a mini boss.
Grants a temporary attack speed increase of 20% for 10s after leaving the Music mode.
Every level of Inspiration received in the Music mode increases the Bard's critical damage by 8% for 10s. The maximum bonus is 40%.

After attaining Inspiration level 300, the Bard, when firing, has a 4% chance to create a chaotic clump of notes that will inflict 100% of the Bard's damage in an area. Damage dealt by the clump is guaranteed to be critical.

Every 10s, the Blade Dancer switches to the dancing state, retaining the attack speed bonus and passing a flower to another Dancer located diagonally. A Dancer with a flower deals 1.6% more damage up to 50 times, and after that 0.75% more. Receiving a flower switches her into the dancing state for 50s, granting a 35% chance for attacks to bounce to a nearby monster.

No longer receives an attack speed bonus when there are no other Blade Dancers nearby. When moving, a Blade Dancer not in dance mode grants a Tower that increases the damage of all Blade Dancers by 2% up to 50 times and by 0.5% after that.
Receiving a flower or moving the Dancer has a 75% chance of removing negative effects from her and neighboring units, and increases the damage dealt by the unit and neighboring units by 10% for 10s.
A Blade Dancer in dance mode gains increased damage to bosses and mini bosses by 30%.
Being in dance mode increases the Blade Dancer's crit chance by 5%.
A Blade Dancer in dance mode has a 20% chance of throwing 3 additional blades at random targets on the field.
While attacking, the Blade Dancer has a 1% chance of summoning a tornado that moves towards monsters and deals 350% of the Blade Dancer's attack damage.
When merging, all Boreas enter the second phase. The interval for triggering the other phases is not reset.
With a 40% chance, the transition to the second phase is reactivated.
Activation of the second phase with a 30% chance will fire two arrows at enemy units, slowing their attack by 25% for 10s in PvP and increasing attack speed in Co-Op.

Activation of the second phase with a 50% chance will unleash a shower of arrows at the monsters, dealing 350 damage. The damage from this attack depends on the merge rank of the unit and is always critical damage.
Every third switching by Boreas to the second phase allows him to attack an additional target until the end of the phase.
Every third switching by Boreas to the second phase increases his damage by 100% until the end of the phase.

With a 30% chance, upon entering the first phase Boreas receives a shield against negative effects until the end of the second phase. There's a 30% chance that moving into the second phase will speed up monsters on the opponent's field by 30%. In Co-Op mode it will slow down the monsters.

Merging and increasing rank near a Bruiser gives him a Clover leaf. Upon collecting a whole Clover, a Bruiser increases his damage by 23% for each Clover and becomes enraged. When collecting 2 whole Clovers, attack speed when enraged increases by 30%. After 50 Clovers, the damage bonus is reduced to 10%.

Merging or absorbing a Bruiser gives a Clover leaf to 3 random Bruisers. Upon collecting a whole Clover, the damage of enraged Bruisers is increased by 10% for each Clover. If 6 or more Clovers are collected, there is a 50% chance that rage will be empowered. After 40 Clovers, the damage bonus is reduced to 2%.

When collecting a Clover and with a 10% chance when collecting a Clover leaf, a Bruiser hurls a mug that leaves a puddle that reduces monsters' armor by 30% to a maximum of 60% and increases received mana by 30%. The puddle exists for 10 sec.
After collecting a whole Clover, a Bruiser cleanses negative effects from 2 random Bruisers.
There is a 5% chance that a Bruiser will deliver a power punch when attacking a boss or mini boss. Such punch deals 500% of the Bruiser's attack damage.
Bruiser damage is increased by 10% for every monster on the path. The maximum bonus is 80%.

The first Bruiser will appear as a Ringleader. As long as there is a Ringleader on the battlefield, all Bruisers will have 5% higher chance of critical damage. If a Ringleader merges, it will jump to the most powerful monster on the path and will instantly kill it. If there are no Ringleaders on the battlefield, a random Bruiser with a low merge rank will become the Ringleader after 30 seconds.
Has a 7% chance when attacking to summon a blizzard around the attacking target that slows targets down. The damage dealt is equal to the Cold Mage's damage.
Frozen targets with health below 30% take 20% more damage.
When the Cold Mage merges, a random unit on the opponent's field is frozen for 6s. Freeze duration increases with the merge rank. Does not work in Co-Op and single player modes.
When merging the Cold Mage, strikes slowed monsters with icicles, pinning frozen monsters to the ground for 1s and dealing 1000 damage. Damage and duration increase in proportion to merge rank.
Every 15 attacks, the Cold Mage freezes monsters for 4s. This effect activates once.
Slows the target by an additional 5% on top of the max amount.
Frozen targets die if their health drops below 5%.

A mage has a 35% chance to appear with a crystal, and merging that mage summons an Arcanist, reducing armor by 0.5% with its attack, up to 75%. If an Arcanist is in a corner or has 2 neighbors, it gains a 70% chain attack. The number of targets depends on its merge rank. Maximum 4 Arcanists.

One mage on the battlefield is always an Arcanist. A mage has a chance of 40% to appear with a crystal, which instead of attacking monsters gives Arcanist a charge of 1.5%. Arcanist has 1000% area damage and attacks only every 3s, spending 3 charges per attack. After 500 charges, its damage increases by 0.4%. Arcanist's critical damage is reduced 3 times.
The chance for a mage to appear with a crystal goes up by 35%. Arcanist's damage increases by 3% for every mage with a crystal on the battlefield.

Increasing rank and merging a mage spawns 2 crystals, which attack the first target and have the same stats as that mage with extra attack bonuses from Arcanist. The crystals appear for 15s and explode when a monster touches them, dealing 1000% of the mage's damage value.

Every 10s, each Arcanist on the battlefield charges its attack with electricity, increasing its critical chance by 100%. This attack leaves ball lightning, dealing 20% of Arcanist's damage value every 0.5s.

When attacking, Arcanist has a 10% chance to unleash sprays of magma that set fire to a monster and its neighbors. Afflicted monsters take 135% of the mage's damage value in periodic damage and 20% increased critical damage from any source. The periodic damage stacks up to 5 times.
After killing a boss, a low rank Arcanist has a 65% chance to absorb its energy and go up in merge rank. If there are no Arcanists on the battlefield, a random mage will rank up.

Cultists receive souls when merging or making a sacrifice. Each soul increases Cultist damage by 2%. When the number of souls reaches 20, a Cultist in demon form transforms into a Harbinger, which increases their attack speed by 15%.

Cultists in demon form receive souls when merging or performing a sacrifice. Every 5 souls, a big guardian demon is summoned. Upon death, the demon sets a random unit on fire, reducing its rank. Summoning 4 guardians transforms a demonic Cultist into a Harbinger which receives a soul every 20 sec. In Co-Op and single player modes, guardians grant mana.
When a Sacrifice is activated, it destroys both Cultists and other units on the field corners. The other units don't grant mana, but they have a 60% chance of granting a soul.
When Sacrifice is activated, every Cultist destroyed will remove the negative effect from 1 random Cultists and will increase its damage by 10% for 10s.
A Cultist in Harbinger form increases its damage by 5% for every Cultist on the field, including Harbingers.

When killing monsters, one Cultist in the Harbinger form will have a 30% chance of summoning fast demons on the enemy's path. When a mini boss or a demon guardian appears on the enemy's path, the Cultist in the form of a Harbinger will increase his damage by 25% for 15s. Does not work in Co-Op and single player modes.

When attacking, Harbinger creates a pulse of fire that lasts 2s. The pulse deals 100% of Cultist's damage value every 0.2s and reduces monster armor in its radius by 40%. There can only be one pulse on the field at a time.

Death of a boss or mini boss will mark a random Hunter of the lowest rank. Merging or absorbing every Hunter with a mark will distribute 3 marks between the Hunters with the highest rank. Damage per mark will increase by 6%, and after 90 marks, it will increase by 1%.

The Demon Hunter no longer gets a bonus for her demonic form. 6 Hunters on the field will become Twilight Hunters, increasing their attack speed by 20%. Once every 10s all the regular Demon Hunters will lose their merge rank, marking Twilight Hunters. Damage per mark will increase by 4.2%, and after 60 marks it will increase by 1%.
There's a 20% chance of the Demon Hunter cleansing herself of negative effects when they are received.
Once per wave, when destroying a boss, it will reduce the merge rank of the highest ranked non-Hunter unit, and will apply marks to 3 multiple times the number.
There's a 7% chance of making an extra attack and inflicting an additional 100% damage to the first target for each merge rank.

When a Phantom appears in PvP, each of the 6 Demon Hunters of the highest merge rank creates a Shadow on the path. It copies the wielder's attack. In Co-Op and single player modes, it triggers when the wave's first boss appears. When a mini boss appears, the Shadow has a 10% chance to appear for every Demon Hunter of the highest merge rank. Damage per mark increases by 6%, after 90 marks by 1%.
The Night Parasite invades the heads of the Demon Hunter's enemies, increasing the chance of critical damage to bosses and mini bosses by 5% for all Hunters.

The Demonologist has a 35% chance of appearing with the Dark Orb. When merging, this kind of Demonologist summons a big Archdemon in front of the opponent's first monster. The Archdemon increases the armor of monsters around it by 30% in PvP mode, or reduces it by 30% in other modes. The Archdemon's health is increased by 30%.
A Demonologist has a 35% chance to appear with a Dark Orb. When merging, this kind of Demonologist summons 2 times more big monsters.
When using Mana Power-up, the Phantom and monsters on the opponent's field gain armor for 6 sec. It reduces the damage received by 30% in PvP and increases the damage by 30% in other modes.
A random Demonologist will receive a Dark Orb either after using a Mana Power-up, or, with a 30% chance, when another Demonologist appears with the Dark Orb.
Merging a Demonologist that has the Dark Orb summons 2 small Imps to the opponent's side. The health of these Imps increases with the Demonologist's merge rank.

When a Demonologist with the Dark Orb is merged, 3 small monsters per Demonologist's merge rank are instantly destroyed. Merging also increases the damage dealt by the units on the field by 1.5%, up to 90%, and increases the health of summoned monsters in PvP by 2.5%.

When a Demonologist with the Dark Orb is merged, 3 small monsters per Demonologist's merge rank are instantly destroyed. Merging also increases the damage dealt by the units on the field by 1.5%, up to 90%, and increases the health of summoned monsters in PvP by 2.5%.
Dryad has a 20% chance of appearing with the blue fairy, increasing the merge rank of this Dryad, and summons a Dryad of the first merge rank.

Dryad has a 20% chance of appearing with a purple fairy. In this case, increasing merge rank with the Dryad's help will also increase damage for all units on the field by 2%. Additional damage will not exceed 50%.
If there are three or more Dryads on the field, the chance of a Dryad appearing with a fairy is increased by 75% of current probability.
With a Mana Power-up, it gives a fairy to a random Dryad without a fairy and has a 40% chance of giving it to another Dryad.

Merging a Dryad who has a fairy with another unit will remove its negative effects and will throw a poisonous fruit at 4 of the opponent's units. The fruit reduces the attack speed of the units by 28% for 30s. In Co-Op mode, it increases attack speed for allied player's units, and in single player modes it increases the attack speed of the player's own units.

Merging a Dryad who has a fairy with another unit will remove its negative effects and will throw invigorating fruit at 4 of the player's own units. The fruit increases the chance of inflicting a critical strike for the player's own units by 2% for 30 seconds.

There's a 25% chance that, instead of an ordinary fairy, the Dryad will receive a golden fairy. When the merge rank of the other units is increased, such a Dryad will increase the merge rank twice. The golden fairy has all the selected fairy effects.

The first Sword on the field will be an Ancient Sword. Merging 8 swords creates a new Ancient Sword. This Sword attacks the first target and steals all boosts with no restrictions, receiving 200% of each boost's power. Its damage is increased by 100% and attack speed is increased by 150%.
An Awakened Sword has a 20% chance to appear on the field. After merging, this Sword applies its color effect to 3 additional targets.
When the Exhaustion effect ends, it has a 30% chance to leave normal weakening on the unit.
Merging a blue Sword has a 15% chance of boosting each target 2 times instead of one.
Each buff has a 35% chance to remove negative effects from the target.
Once per wave, when the boss is killed, a random blue and random red Sword apply their color effects.

Merging units under the effects of the Sword has a 20% chance of transferring them to another unit. Additionally, every red Sword increases damage to all player units by 1%, while the blue Sword increases the attack speed of all units by 1% for every merge rank of that color. Ancient Swords include swords of both colors and get 50% of each bonus.

Upon appearing, and then once every 30s, the Engineer drops a part that will give a random Engineer and the Engineers near him an attack for a period of 10s, inflicting 40% damage in the area around the target.
Upon appearing, and then once every 30s, the Engineer drops a part that will increase the attack speed of a random Engineer and the Engineers near to him by 70% for 10s.
Upon hit, the part has a 35% chance of casting a shield on the Engineer, providing protection against one negative effect.
There's a 15% chance of a part being able to ricochet into a random Engineer. After the boss appears, there's a 35% chance that the Engineer will drop a part.
The maximum bonus from connections is achieved with 10 engineers in the chain.
If there are 13 or more engineers in the chain, the bonus from connections is increased by 30%, and gives an extra 5% chance of critical damage.
Merging will now also spawn a part. In addition, the part has a 10% chance of increasing the Engineer's merge rank.

Franky has a 40% chance of being charged when spawning. This kind of Franky reduces the armor of monsters in her area by 12% per merge rank, but only up to a maximum of 60%. Merging a charged Franky summons a charged Robo-Ducky to the opponent's path. Upon death, the Robo-Ducky paralyzes 3 enemy units for 2 seconds. Merging 2 charged Frankies with each other always results in a charged Franky.

Franky has a 40% chance of becoming toxic, capable of biting the units of the same or lower rank, reducing their rank and summoning a monster. Mana Power-up increases the enemies' armor by 0.3% per bolt for 10 sec (max. of 30%). In Co-Op, armor is reduced instead. Merging 2 toxic Frankies always results in a toxic Franky. Max. 5 toxic Frankies.
When merging, Franky has a 100% chance to cleanse a negative effect from a random Franky on the field.
Killing a small summoned monster on the opponent's field has a 35% chance to grant a bolt to Stein.
A toxic or charged Franky of merge rank 4 or above increases the rate of sending signals to Stein by 40%.
Merging a toxic or charged Franky summons 2 armored big monsters. The armor of these monsters absorbs 60% of damage. In Co-Op, damage is not absorbed.

After receiving 60 bolts, Stein increases his chance to deal critical damage by 4%, and the movement-induced damage increases by 100%. Upon receiving 100 bolts, Stein gains his final form, increasing the damage he deals by 50%.

With a 50% chance, the Frost will appear with a Mark of Ice. Merging a Frost of this type with another unit reduces the amount of mana the opponent is receiving by 60% for 4s. In Co-Op and single player modes, increases the amount of mana the player receives.

There's a 50% chance of Frost appearing with the Mark of Ice. Merging this Frost with another unit will freeze all monsters on the path for 3 sec, and apply 8 frostbite charges. Freezing will prevent bosses and mini bosses using abilities.
Frost with the Mark of Ice with a 4 merge rank and higher, has a 10% chance of using merge effects when a blizzard appears on its half of the field.
The duration of the Frost's merge effects is extended by 33%.

Merging a Frost with the Mark of Ice with another unit covers the opponent's path with ice for 5s, destroying all traps on the path and increasing the speed of the monsters on the ice by 25%. Does not work in Co-Op and single player modes.
Merging Frost with a Mark of Ice protects the opponent's monsters from 40% of damage for 5s. In Co-Op and single-player modes, it damages armor by 40%.

One of the Frosts will become the Frost King and receive the Mark of Ice after 10s. Every 10s, the Frost King freezes an opponent's unit for 5s. Every 3 merge ranks increase the number of units by 1. In Co-Op and single player modes, it freezes monsters except for bosses.

Two of the Genies on the field are always Genie Sultans. Increasing the damage dealt by neighboring Genies by 5%, and by the additional 5% for every merge rank. A Genie Sultan participating in forming a line grants an additional charge.

All the player's Genies turn into Ifrits. Their damage is increased by 45%, and vortexes reduce their targets' armor by 30%. An Ifrit that is at the end of a line has a 50% chance to burst into flames and burn for 6 sec, giving a buff charge for every merge rank.
At the start of the game, the Genie creates an enchanted tile on the player's field. Using a Mana Power-up turns a unit on that tile into a Genie.
Using a Mana Power-up cleanses 3 Genies from negative effects with a 50% chance per each Genie.
Vortex damage increased by 35%.
Damage dealt by activated Genies' basic attacks is increased by 45%.

Now, Mana Power-ups can be used twice before their cooldown is triggered. After acquiring 100 charges, Magic Fists will deal additional damage equal to 5% of the target's current health. At 200 charges, the Genie's critical attack chance increases by 5%.
When a neighboring unit is attacking, there is a 4% chance that the Grindstone will spawn sparks that hit a random monster and do area damage. The damage is 200% of the Grindstone's damage.

Every time a unit next to the Grindstone attacks, the Grindstone spawns a spark that does 8% of its damage to a single target. When units next to the Grindstone merge or increase their merge rank, the damage done by the Grindstone's sparks increases by 25%.
There is a 50% chance that spawning sparks will generate one more spark.
The damage dealt by the Grindstone to multiple targets is no longer reduced, leaving it at 100% of the damage it deals to single targets.

When a Grindstone is merged or increases its merge rank, all Grindstones overheat to increase their damage by 10% for 10 seconds. With each transition into the overheated state, the effect duration increases by 2 seconds, while the damage bonus increases by 1.5%.
When 3 Grindstones are next to each other, they overheat and increase the damage they deal by 30%.
The Grindstone's critical chance is increased by 1% for every merge rank.
Ghost Shot increases critical damage against the target by 20%.

The Gunslinger deals area damage. Moving Gunslingers switches them into the Shootout mode for 5s. Ghost Shots do not destroy armor, but the Gunslingers' damage in the Shootout mode is increased by 300%. When moving a Gunslinger of rank 2+ into a special tile, he loses 1 rank and for each of his ranks, gains x2 charges that increase the damage from basic attacks by 1%.
After moving, the Gunslinger has a 40% chance of clearing any negative effect.
Monsters under the effect of the Ghost Shot bring 20% more mana.
The Ghost Shot links targets together, transferring 5% of the total damage received between them. Damage from area attacks will not be transferred more than once.
Units adjacent to the Gunslinger receive a 8% attack speed increase.

The movement cooldown time is reduced to 5s, and monsters under the effect of armor destruction from Ghost Shot have an extra 3% chance of receiving critical damage. In the Shootout mode, all damage dealt by Gunslingers is increased by 30%.

Every copying by the Harlequin gives you a playing card. If a specified amount of cards is accumulated by the end of a wave, the 20% bonus to the damage of all units on the field, and the 10% bonus to the mana received for the next wave, are activated for all units on the field. After receiving 5 cards, the Harlequin exchanges them for mana equal to 100% of the summoning cost.

Every copying by the Harlequin gives you a playing card. The copy created by the Harlequin reverts to the Harlequin form after 20s. After receiving 5 cards, the Harlequin exchanges them for mana equal to 100% of the summoning cost.
If there are no units with the Harlequin's weakening, or there is more than 4 of them on the field, all units receive a 20% bonus to critical damage.
With a 40% chance, when receiving a card, the Harlequin will give one more.
When copying, the Harlequin smacks a random monster with a hammer, dealing damage equivalent to 10% of the target's health to all monsters around the target.

When a unit is merged while being weakened by the Harlequin, a trap appears on the path. When a monster comes in contact with the trap, a Jack will spring out of it, lowering the monsters' armor by 75% and blocking them from using abilities for 1.5s. Jack's effects do not work on the same monster twice. The trap exists for 15s and vanishes when activated.
The Harlequin may now copy units of his own rank or lower. When copying, his rank is lowered to that of the unit being copied.

Killing a boss or mini boss gets you a trophy and summons a fast boar to your opponent's field. Each trophy increases the boar's health by 4%. When the Hunter kills a common monster, there is also a 8% chance of summoning the boar. Does not work in Co-Op and single player modes.

When a Hunter kills a boss or a mini boss, or with a 5% chance when he kills a common monster, a trophy is earned, increasing this unit's damage by 4%. The damage bonus after 60 trophies is reduced to 0.5%. When attacking, has a 7% chance of firing an acorn at a random target and inflicting increased damage.
Moves to the next target every 15 shots.
Has a 50% chance to also deal 150% more damage with the first shot.
Hunters at merge rank 3 or higher deal 50% damage in a small radius around the target.
Hunters at merge rank 3 or higher get an additional 10% critical damage chance.
Killing a boss or mini boss has a 20% chance of increasing the merge rank of one Hunter.

Instead of an Inquisitor, Knights of Light appear on the field. Knights of Light receive a 3.5% damage increase for each boss killed. Additionally, merging any Inquisitor activates Knights of Light for 10 seconds.

The first Inquisitor on the field will appear as a Knight of Darkness. A Knight of Darkness is always active and steals a merge rank from other Inquisitors on the field every 12 seconds, increasing its own damage by 6% for each rank absorbed. The bonus is reduced by 3% after you achieve 30 rank absorptions.
When Inquisitors activate, they have a 50% chance to remove negative effects from Knights. Activating them also increases their speed by 10% for 10 sec.
Knights appear on the field with a shield that protects them against one negative effect. If there are no other Inquisitors on the field, the Knight will restore her shield in 3.5 seconds.
If a Knight is alone on the field, her maximum damage limit increases to 1100%.

If there are 4+ Inquisitors on the field, their damage increases by 20%. If there are 7+ Inquisitors, their critical damage also increases by 35%. If there are 10+ Inquisitors, their critical chance also increases by 10%.
When attacking, active Knights have a 3% chance to summon a Hammer of Faith that stuns the target for 4 sec and deals 777% damage. Once stunned, a target cannot be stunned again.

The Statue boosts affect all units in the same row (vertical or horizontal), additionally increasing their damage for each appearance of another Statue in the row by 1.2% up to 30 times, and then by 0.3%. The statue is active if there are no other Statues in the row, regardless of the number of Statues on the field.
Each merge and increase in merge rank near a Statue increases the damage of its neighbors by 1.2% up to 30 times, and then by 0.3%.
The Knight Statue appears with a shield against negative effects. The shield has 2 charges. Once destroyed, the shield regenerates in 30s.
When one Knight Statue gets a bonus damage charge, other Statues have a 15% chance of receiving that bonus as well. This chance is unique to each statue.
If all of Knight Statue's neighbors are the same unit, then, in addition to attack speed, it will also increase their critical damage by 15%.
In addition to attack speed, Knight Statue increases unit critical damage by 1% for each merge rank of Statues on the battlefield.

While active, Knight Statue performs an area attack every 5s. The attack deals 50% of its critical damage value per merge rank and reduces monster armor by 25%. Its attack interval goes down 1s for every unit neighboring it.
After producing ore, the Mine has a 50% chance to become a Rich Mine. This kind of mine gives 3 ore per rank.

Kobolds that are in the Mine tiles vanish only after using the Mana Power-up. The amount of ore received depends on their merge ranks — respectively 1, 4, 9, 16, 25, 36, 49. Mana Power-up only moves the Mines that have no Kobolds in them.
When receiving ore, there is a 40% chance of a Kobold of merge rank 1 spawning in a random tile.
When a Kobold's merge rank increases, there is a 50% chance to increase the merge rank of a random Kobold of the lowest merge rank on the field.
When a Kobold is merged or absorbed, he throws a pick at the first monster on the path. All monsters in the area where the pick lands receive 30% extra critical damage. The effect does not stack.

The critical damage of all units on the field is increased by 3.5% for each merge rank of the Kobold that has the highest merge rank. Only the 1 Kobold may give this bonus. Moving the mine via the Mana Power-up does not affect at least one Kobold with the highest merge rank.

After reaching the maximum amount of ore, 1 mine turns into a Diamond Mine. After ore is gathered in the Diamond Mine, all units on the field have their chance of dealing critical damage increased by 2% for 30 sec.
As the Cauldron appears, it produces an elixir that gives 20 mana to a random unit on the field. If you get 3 elixirs into the Cauldron, it will start generating mana at a faster rate for 10 secs.
As the Cauldron appears, it produces an elixir that increases a unit's damage by 25% for 15 sec. If you get 3 elixirs into the Cauldron, it gives a double bonus to neighboring units for 20 secs.
If the Cauldron is placed on the field, and the elixir gets into it, a cooling potion is released that inflicts 600 damage (depending on merge rank) and slows monsters down by 25%.
If the Cauldron is placed on the field, and the elixir gets into it, a magic potion is produced that inflicts 600 damage (depending on merge rank) and increases mana for killing monsters by 6.
Elixir getting into the cauldron with a 30% chance releases another elixir to a random unit on the field.
When generating mana, the cauldron has a 5% chance of creating an elixir.
With a 5% chance, the elixir created by the Cauldron will increase the merge rank of the unit by one. Besides, merging Cauldrons produces elixirs.

There's a 45% chance of a Meteor appearing on the battlefield with a Flame Orb. When merging, this Meteor summons a comet that instantly kills small monsters in a small area and damages big monsters for 20% of their health per merge rank.

A Meteor will have a 45% chance to appear on the field with a Flame Orb. Such a meteor will cause a bolide to appear when it merges. Killing monsters with a Bolide grants a magma charge that increases the Meteor's damage by 2.2% and the Meteorite damage by 1.5% for each monster killed with the Bolide. Grants x5 magma charges for bosses and mini bosses. Maximum 700 charges.
The Comet and the Bolide set monsters on fire, dealing 10% of the Meteorite damage 1 times per second and increasing the critical damage received by monsters by 20%.
When a Meteor appears with a Flame Orb there's a 20% chance of giving another Flame Orb to another random Meteor.

A meteor of merge rank 3+ possessing a Flame Orb, when attacking, has a 10% chance to cause an explosion that deals 25% of the Meteorite's damage in a small area around the target. The chance to deal critical damage with this explosion is 20%.
Causes a second explosion in the spot where a Comet or a Bolide has landed, dealing damage equal to 15% of the monsters' missing health.

When merged, the Meteor has a 20% chance to summon an additional Meteorite, Comet or Bolide. Merging Meteors with Flame Orbs also gives a 15% chance to grant an extra Flame Orb to another random Meteor.

When the Minotaur is merged, appears on the field or increases rank, a Fury charge is added, and all Minotaurs go into the Berserker mode for 15s. While in the Berserker mode, the damage done by the earthquakes is increased by 75%, and for extra 3% per each Fury charge (or for 0.5% after 300 charges).

When the Minotaur is merged, appears on the field or increases rank, a Fury charge is added. Whenever a Fury charge is added or a boss appears, all Minotaurs go into the Berserker mode for 20s, which increases attack speed by 400%, while also granting area attack and bonus damage equal to 100% of the earthquake's damage. Fury charges increase the damage dealt by 1.25% (or by 0.25% after 300 charges).
When the Minotaur appears on the field, he stuns all the monsters on his path for 2s. Once a target has been stunned, it may not be stunned again.
When the Minotaur appears, he stuns 3 units on the opponent's field for 3s. Does not work in Co-Op and single player modes.
The Earthquake damage leaves a Crack effect on the monster, which increases the damage inflicted by the earthquake for each of these effects by 5%. Maximum damage cannot exceed 300%.
With each main attack, the damage of the Minotaur and his summoned Earthquakes is increased by 10%. Maximum damage cannot exceed 400%. Bonus damage is reset when the target is changed.
The Minotaur's critical damage chance is increased for each Minotaur on the field by 0.4%. Additionally increases critical chance in Berserker mode by 4%.

Monks appear on the battlefield with an orb of Power. Every orb of power increases a Monk's damage by 6%. When such a Monk is merged or absorbed, all of his orbs are transferred to a random Monk positioned in the intersection. If none are found, then the orbs will go to any other Monk.

A random Monk periodically receives a balance sphere. Each time a unit with this sphere merges or ranks up, all Monks increase their damage by 9%; after 50 the bonus is reduced to 2%. After a Monk moves or merges, if a unit with a balance sphere does not merge as well within 10%, the sphere disappears. A Monk at an intersection of lines automatically absorbs the sphere.
Activating a boost will remove negative effects from Monks in intersections.
Activating a buff has a 50% chance to increase the rank of a random Monk.
During each Power-up, the first Monk merge will turn a random non-Monk unit into a Monk.
After each buff, the first merge of a Monk summons a Monk to the battlefield from merge rank 1 to 3 and produces an additional sphere.
Gaining a buff increases Monk's critical damage chance by 5% and increase Monk's area attack bonus at intersections to 50%.

When merging, a random Phoenix gains the Mark of Fire. Such a Phoenix, when entering active state, receives 2 times the amount of Flame Particles and, with 100% chance, 1 Charge of Awakening, before losing the Mark. After 100 Flame Particles, the Phoenix's critical damage increases by 20%.

No longer becomes inactive, and the way of gaining the Flame Particles is changed. Tapping the Phoenix will Immolate him and grant 4 Flame Particles per every merge rank. After 100 Flame Particles, the Phoenix's critical damage increases by 35%.
A random Phoenix cleanses himself of all negative effects, with a 80% chance upon becoming active, and a 100% chance upon immolating.
Summons a Phoenix of merge rank 1 on the field, with a 30% chance upon becoming active, and a 40% chance upon immolating.
Upon immolating or becoming active this Phoenix increases the damage of neighbouring Phoenixes by 50% for 20s.
If there are no other Phoenixes next to this Phoenix, his damage is increased by 50%.
Getting 300 Flame Particles will increase the Phoenix's critical damage chance by 5%.

When teleporting, has a 8% chance of warping a monster and sending it to the opponent's path as a small warped monster. The player receives mana for killing the monster, but the opponent does not. Does not work on bosses or mini bosses. In Co-Op and single player modes, lowers the defense of the teleported monster by 20%.
Instead of standard area damage, all teleported monsters except for bosses and mini bosses now deal damage within range equal to 2.2% of the teleported monster's current health.
Teleported or warped monsters will give 20% more mana.
Teleported monsters take 20% more critical damage. Critical damage will be 20% less for warped monsters on the opponent's side.

All teleported monsters rapidly age after the teleport: their defense decreases by 25% and their speed decreases by 30%. Warped monsters on the opponent's side become younger; their defense increases by 25% and their speed increases by 30%.
When merging the Portal Mage, all teleported monsters fall into the portal net. 7% of all damage dealt to monsters in the net will be transferred to other connected monsters.

When a boss or mini boss is teleported, a big Portal Healer is summoned to the opponent's side. The healer restores 2.5% of max health to monsters within a small radius every 3 sec. When the Portal Healer dies, he restores 7% of lost health to monsters. Only works in PvP mode.
When she appears, there's a 45% chance of creating 2 special tiles on the field. Merging inside these tiles will grant 150 mana. This ability cannot be activated more than 2 times in 20s.

When merging, Priestesses will throw a bottle of Holy Water at the monster on the battlefield. The bottle will leave a puddle that inflicts 200 damage per merge rank and reduces the monsters' armor by 30%. The puddle will last for 15s.
The death or merging of a Priestess has a 20% chance to give mana twice.
Gives 40 mana when placed on the field.
After receiving any mana from a Priestess, all Priestesses on the field receive a buff for 6s, which increases damage by 150% in proportion to rank and gives 50% area attack.

After receiving any mana from a Priestess, all Priestesses on the field receive a buff for 6s, which increases damage by 150% in proportion to rank and gives 50% area attack. The Priestess also speeds up by 30%.
Receiving mana from the Priestess increases the attack speed of units on the field by 15% for 6s. The Priestess also speeds up by 30%.

Instant killing of a monster by a Reaper will give you a trophy skull, which increases the damage inflicted by all Reapers by 3% and summons a ghostly reaper to the player's side, instead of bringing a ghost to the opponent's side. This type of ghost will move towards monsters and inflict 200% of the Reaper's damage in the area around itself. The bonus is reduced to 0.3% after 150 trophies.

Activation of the instant kill has a 30% chance to inflict damage in the area around the target, with an instant kill chance 2.5 times lower than the basic level. The big ghost resulting from this type of kill will be 100% stronger.
When attacking bosses and mini bosses, the Reaper's activated abilities deal 500% more damage.
Instantly killing a monster with the Reaper's attack gives 3 mana. The amount of mana increases based on the Reaper's merge rank.
The attack has a 10% chance of causing the scythe to ricochet at a random boss or mini boss on the path.

Increases the chance of summoning ghosts by 25% and their movement speed by 50%. Merging Reapers activates instant kill of 2 common monsters in proportion to rank. Ghosts cannot be summoned in Co-Op and single player modes.
Merging the Reaper increases the attack speed of other Reapers on the field by 25% for 10s.

There is a 35% chance that a Dubious Riding Hood might appear on the field instead of an ordinary Riding Hood. The Dubious Riding Hood produces 100% more mana. Damage from all Wolves increases by 4% for every merge rank of all Dubious Riding Hoods on the field.

The charge counter becomes shared. In the empowered form, charges increase damage by 1.5% up to 40, and by 0.5% after that. New abilities can be received at 1, 5, 20, and 40 charges, but the Wolf form is temporary and only lasts 50 sec after absorbing a unit.
Receiving a temporary power-up will stun an opposing unit on the corresponding tile for 5s. Does not work in Co-Op or single-player modes.
Absorbing units has a 75% chance of clearing a unit of negative effects.

When the Riding Hood is in the Wolf form, damage from the opponent's units is reduced by 3%. The effectiveness of damage reduction is reduced with every Wolf on the field, down to 2% per unit. In Co-Op, the effect increases allied units' damage. In single-player modes, it increases your own units' damage.
The Riding Hood scares nearby enemies, lowering their armor by 3%, and by an additional 3% for every merge rank. The lowering effects of several Riding Hoods do not stack.
When the Riding Hood appears on the field, she will feed a random Wolf, increasing its critical damage chance by 5% and giving mana depending on the unit's merge rank.
Gives a 10% chance to increase the Robot's merge rank when performing the upgrade. Gains access to a new upgrade at 14 Parts, increasing the critical attack chance by 5% and the damage by 25%.

After 2 merges, each Robot performs an upgrade and gains a Wrench. The Mana Power-up now spends 1 Wrench and triggers a volley of missiles dealing 100% of the Robot's critical damage in the area. Gains access to a new upgrade at 14 Parts, increasing the critical attack chance by 5%.
Using a Wrench gives a 50% chance to remove negative effects from a random Robot.
When performing an upgrade, has a 40% chance to perform another upgrade.
Increases the critical damage of a Robot of 6+ merge rank by 2.5% per Part, but only up to the maximum of 70%.
Using the Wrench's buff increases the attack speed of all Robots with the Fine Calibrations talent and all Robots with the General Program talent by 40% for 20s.

Gains access to a new upgrade at 20 Parts, starting to fire a laser at the first target with a 10% chance on attacking. The laser deals 400% damage to common monsters and 800% damage to bosses and mini bosses.

There's a 50% chance of the Rogue being equipped with thorns. During merging, appearing or changing merge rank, it throws them at the first target. For 10s, the thorns inflict 250% of the Rogue's damage to monsters passing over them, and slow them down.

There's a 50% chance of the Rogue being equipped with thorns. During merging, appearing or changing merge rank, it throws them at the first target. For 10s, the spikes increase the damage inflicted on monsters in the area around them by 75%.
There's a 35% chance of the Rogue using a smoke bomb. During merging, appearance or changing of merge rank it shrouds all Rogues in smoke for 5s, increasing their damage by 20%.
There's a 35% chance of the Rogue using an invigorating bomb. During merging, appearance or changing of merge rank it shrouds all units in smoke for 5s, increasing their attack speed by 15%.
While in the smoke, the Rogue attacks the first 3 target(s).
While in the smoke, the Rogue attacks the target twice.

There's a 15% chance of the Shinobi appearing instead of the Rogue, and being equipped with both a bomb and spikes. A random value between 0 and 40% of critical damage is added to the additional damage. In addition, once per 20s he shrouds himself and neighboring Rogues in smoke and throws spikes at monsters. Any Rogue shrouded in smoke is protected from negative effects.

A random Scrapper is turned into a Recycler if there are no Recyclers on the battlefield yet. When absorbing a unit, the Recycler increases the damage dealt by every unit on the field by 20% for 10s. The mana ability will be directed at the Recycler now, until its rank is reduced to 1.

Gains the ability to absorb units of a lower rank. The Mana ability reduces the rank of Scrappers with the lowest rank, but only if there is more than 1 Scrapper on the field, and gives mana equal to 100% of the summoning cost.
Merging a Scrapper has a 15% chance of increasing a random unit's rank out of order.
When merging a Scrapper, the result is guaranteed to be a Scrapper as well.
Merging the Scrapper sends a big Sticky Slime to the opponent's side. The Slime has 500% of a common monster's health. Does not work in Co-Op or single player modes.

When a Scrapper absorbs a unit, a caustic pool appears on the path, and will move towards the monsters. A monster's armor is reduced by 10% when coming into contact with this trap. The armor-reducing effect of several pools stacks up to 2 times.
Has a 30% chance for the bonus enhancement to be applied twice and for the Scrapper to increase the merge rank of 3 units instead of 2.

When you receive treasure, a ghost will haunt 1 random opponent unit, paralyzing it for 3.5s. The paralysis effect bypasses shields and cannot be removed by cleansing. After 20 treasure is accumulated, an additional target will be paralyzed. Doesn't work in Co-Op or single-player modes. Max treasure +10.

There's a 15% chance of the Captain appearing on the battlefield instead of the Sea Dog. When the Captain appears, one treasure is buried. Captains of merge rank 4 or higher get an additional 30% damage. Max treasure +10.

Sea Dogs of merge rank 4 or higher will dig up treasure on their tile when any other treasure is received. Treasure above the maximum will go to a random strongest Sea Dog, with 3% damage for each one. Max treasure +10.

When you receive regular treasure, your opponent will be attacked by an Angler, which has 500% of the health of a regular monster, giving 200% mana to the player who summoned it. When you receive rare treasure, an Angler mini boss will be summoned with the same bonuses. Doesn't work in Co-Op or single-player modes. Max treasure +10.

When you receive rare treasure, 1 bomb will be buried on the opponent's battlefield. If a unit appears, merges or increases its rank on a tile with a bomb, there will be an explosion, which will reduce the merge rank of the unit on the tile. Does not work in Co-Op or single-player modes. Max treasure +10.

After finding rare treasure bosses and mini bosses will appear with a powder keg, which will explode after 1s and inflict damage equal to 10% of their current health. Damage to bosses and mini bosses is reduced by 2 times. Receiving rare treasure increases damage by 10%, up to 40%. Max treasure +10.

There's a 10% chance of a piece of amulet being buried. Each piece received will increase the damage inflicted by all Sea Dogs by 5%. When 4 pieces are collected, every 20s the strongest Sea Dog will summon a frigate, to attack random monsters on the battlefield. Each cannonball's damage equals 500% of the unit's damage. Max treasure +10.

4 corner tiles become the Sentry's Guard Posts. A Sentry standing on such a tile becomes a Spotter. The Spotter's Enhanced Bolts attack the first target. When hit with a Spotter's Enhanced Bolt, the target gains a mark that increases the damage inflicted by the Sentries' Enhanced Bolts by 15%. The maximum bonus to damage is 300%.

2 central tiles adjacent to the path become the Sentry's Guard Posts. The damage from Sentries' Enhanced Bolts increases by 20% when a Sentry is moved to a Guard Post (or appears there). The damage bonus is reduced to 2% after getting a Sentry onto Guard Posts 9 times. Every 15s, the tiles move around.
A Sentry standing at a Guard Post has a 40% chance to shoot 2 additional Enhanced Bolts at random monsters.

Every time a Sentry appears at a Guard Post, all Sentries have their attack speed increased by 50% for 10s. If the bonus is received again during that time, the effect is renewed for 10s, and the attack speed bonus becomes equal to 100%.
Enhanced Bolts from the Sentries standing at Guard Posts additionally explode, inflicting area damage and shattering enemy armor by 15% in a small radius around the point of impact.
The Sentries' Enhanced Bolts gain a 5% bonus chance to inflict critical damage.

There is a 20% chance that a Ballista-wielding Sentry will appear on the field. This Sentry's Enhanced Bolts deal 200% extra damage. Merging any Sentry will cause all Ballistas on the field to fire. A Ballista's shot is guaranteed to deal critical damage, and the number of targets is in proportion to the merge rank of the Ballista-wielding Sentry.
The Crazy Shooting phase gives the Sharpshooter 75% area damage and is activated when fighting mini bosses for 4s.
Has a 7% chance to deal an additional 600% damage.

After a boss or mini boss has been killed, the Sharpshooter fires a special projectile at a random unit owned by the opponent, stunning it for 4s or 1s respectively. Does not work in Co-Op mode or in the Plunder trial.

The Sharpshooter shoots shrapnel when a boss or mini boss appears. Shrapnel briefly paralyzes monsters, lowering their armor by 30%, and deals 100% periodic damage for 4s. Damage increases with merge rank.
Damage to bosses or mini bosses is increased by 5%.
The first attack by the Sharpshooter increases critical damage dealt to bosses or mini bosses from any source by 40%.
During the Crazy Shooting phase, the Sharpshooter lowers the target's armor by 0.25% down to 40%.
Upon a Mana Power-Up assumes the wind spirit form for 15s, increasing attack speed against bosses and mini bosses for 30%.

Upon a Mana Power-Up assumes the fire spirit form for 15s, gaining a 30% AoE attack and increasing damage by 2% for each Spirit Master. Upon decreasing, his Mana Power-up level drops to 1 bypassing intermediate levels of Mana Up.
Upon a Mana Power-Up of 6 or higher has a 30% chance to refund mana spent for the Power-up and a 25% chance to increase merge rank of one Spirit Master.
Upon a Mana Power-Up of 6 or higher the Spirit Master has a 75% chance to clean negative effects from itself.
After assuming the spirit form, the Spirit Master gains a 7% chance to throw a boulder dealing additional 200% attack damage in small area.

When using Mana Power-up, the Spirit Master throws a rock at random monsters, dealing 200% of the unit's damage, stunning monsters for 2 sec, and reducing their armor by 2.5%. The armor reduction effect can stack up to 20%. Stun cannot be triggered repeatedly.
A Mana Power-up of 6 or higher increases the Spirit Master's crit chance by 2%. A Mana Power-up of 9 or higher additionally increases his crit chance by 2%.
There's a 30% chance of the Summoner becoming an Oracle. When merging, the Oracle will summon 2 units. Besides, the unit replacement can be applied up to 3 times without needing to recharge.

There's a 30% chance of the Summoner becoming an Oracle. When merging the Oracle the merge rank of the summoned unit will be 1 higher. Additionally, replacement will now turn a random Summoner of the highest rank into another unit.
With a 20% chance after the appearance of Summoner and 100% chance after the appearance of Oracle, the cost of summoning the next enemy unit goes up by 20%, but not more than 1 times per summoning.
With a 20% chance after the appearance of Summoner and 100% chance after the appearance of Oracle, the cost of summoning your next unit goes down by 30%, but not more than 1 times per summoning.

Merging the Oracle accumulates spirits. When a unit is summoned by the Summoner, all of the player's units receive a 2% damage bonus per each accumulated spirit for 10s. The duration is increased by 0.5s for each spirit. After 10 spirits, there is a 20% chance of activating the bonus when any unit appears. The maximum number of spirits is 40.

Merging the Oracle will accumulate spirits. When the Summoner summons a unit, it sends small evil flamelings to the opponent, in the numbers proportional to merge rank. When dying, a flameling reduces the speed of the opponent's units by 25% for 5 sec. The duration is increased by 0.3 sec for each spirit. After 10 spirits, it becomes possible to activate them using replacements.

With each 2 spirits received, the player's monster critical damage is increased by 30% and the opponent's monster critical damage is reduced by 15%. The effect lasts for 8s. When 10 spirits have been accumulated, the effect works every time you get a spirit.
Tesla's damage in the chain is increased by 1.2% per merge rank in proportion to the number of Teslas in the chain. The maximum bonus is achieved with 8 Teslas in the chain.

If there is no Professor on the field, he will appear when a Tesla merges. Does not attack, but increases the damage of all Teslas on the field by 0.48% per his charge (0.05% after 68 charges) in proportion to his merge rank. He doesn't have a charge limit. Receives a charge when any Tesla is fully charged. When destroyed, the Professor's charges are saved and given to him when he reappears.
Merging a unit next to a Tesla has a 50% chance of removing negative effects from 2 Teslas.
Charges over the limit have a 100% chance to be sent to a random uncharged Tesla.
Each charged Tesla increases the damage of all Teslas by 4%.
With a chance of 2.3% per rank, Tesla's attacks may cause a lightning strike, dealing additional damage equal to 100% of Tesla's damage.
A charged Tesla of merge rank 7 may gain an additional charge which increases his damage by 13%. Charged Teslas' critical damage chance is increased by 4%.

The Trapper has a 50% chance to appear on the field as the Huntmaster and additionally throw a mantrap at a boss or mini boss on the path — or, if there are none, at a random monster. The mantrap has the bonuses of the nets and also increases the critical damage against monsters in the effect's radius by 5%. The effect stacks up to 20%.

The Trapper has a 50% chance to become the Huntmaster, throwing mantraps instead of nets. Mantraps have the same bonuses as nets and also increase the damage dealt to monsters by traps and creatures on the path by 10%, up to 40%. The mantrap also deals damage to monsters once.
The Huntmaster has a 30% chance to throw a mantrap again.
The mantrap's effect radius increases by 15%.

When the Mana Power-up is activated, a random Trapper throws a bush in front of big monsters on the opponent's field. In PvP mode, bushes increase the armor of monsters by 15%, while in Co-Op and single player modes they reduce it by 15%.
When Mana Power-up is activated, summons a Tough Guy to the opponent's side. Has a 33% chance to summon a Blazing Burn instead of the Tough Guy. Does not work in Co-Op and single player modes.
When a boss appears, a random Trapper throws a projectile that stops the boss for 2 sec and links it with other bosses. Linked monsters transfer 10% of the damage received to each other.
Treants of merge rank 4 gain the ability to accumulate leaves and a 60% bonus to critical damage. Every leaf adds 7.8% to damage dealt. After 30 leaves, the bonus is reduced to 3.9%.

Treants of merge rank 4 gain the ability to accumulate leaves, while also increasing the damage dealt by all Treants by 7%. Each leaf adds 1.5% to the damage increase. After 70 leaves, the bonus is reduced to 0.75%.
When receiving a negative effect, has a 50% chance to be cleansed of them.
The number of leaves received from using mana power-up has a 40% chance to be doubled.
Upon receiving a leaf, the Treant spreads barbed roots that deal 200% damage of the Treants every 0.5s for 5s.

Every 15th time the Treant attacks, it throws a toxic mushroom at a random monster, briefly stunning them and dealing 300% damage. Bosses and mini bosses are priority targets. Stunned targets cannot be stunned again.
When receiving a leaf, the Treant has a 20% chance to receive 1 more leaf. If there are 4 Treants of Rank 4 or above on the field, all Treants increase their chance of dealing critical damage by 5%.
After claiming 50 Souls, the Twilight Ranger's attack speed is increased by 70%, and each of this Ranger's attacks has a 2% chance to instantly kill a small monster.
The Twilight Ranger increases the Magical Shot's target area by 30%. After gaining 50 souls, the damage from the Magical Shot increases by 200%.
A random Twilight Ranger will be cleansed of negative effects upon the Soul transfer.
Instantly killing a monster with the Twilight Ranger's ability has a 40% chance to grant a Soul.
After claiming 50 Souls, the Twilight Ranger has a 10% chance to perform 4 additional shots against random targets when attacking. Each target receives 100% of the Twilight Ranger's damage.
When the Twilight Ranger claims 50 Souls, her critical damage increases by 35%.
When the Twilight Ranger claims 150 Souls, increases her chance of dealing critical damage by 4%, and the damage from the Magical Shot by 30%.

If there is an equal number of Twins of different colors on the field, they attain the state of balance for 30 sec: each attack reduces the armor of monsters by 1.5%, but only to a maximum of 30%. For every 2 ranks, a Twin brings an additional Particle.

Merging does not give particles, and the form bonuses depend on the number of units (the maximum bonus is at 11 Twins). When there's an equal number of different colors, they enter the state of balance for 30 sec, increasing the damage they deal by 60%. Mana Power-up used for units in the state of balance grants particles equal to the number of pairs of different colors, resets the boost duration.
Upon attaining balance, a random Twin has a 100% chance to be cleansed of negative effects. Merging Twins of different colors additionally grants 1 particle.
Twins of merge rank 6+ deal 35% more critical damage.
Twins with neighbors of the opposite color and no neighbors of their own color gain a 30% bonus to critical damage.
Twins of merge rank 6+ deal 35% more critical damage.

If a Twin of merge rank 7 is to be destroyed, he loses 1 rank instead of dying. The twins' critical chance is increased by 2.5% while the balance bonus lasts. Upon reaching 100 charges, the critical chance bonus is doubled.

When a Chosen One is lost, all Valkyries on the battlefield have their attack speed increased by 10% for 35 sec. After 70 runes, the attack speed bonus increases to 20%, and the damage increases by 50%.

Three Valkyries on the field become Leaders. Mana Power-up ranks up the Leader of the lowest merge rank, while all other Valkyries will be destroyed, granting Runes. A Chosen One grants 2 times the amount of runes, and losing her summons an extra Valkyrie of merge rank 1. Runes are granted only if the Mana Power-up button is tapped. Leaders have their damage increased by 200%, and they are the only ones to be affected with the Runes.
Losing a Valkyrie has a 40% chance to cleanse a random Valkyrie of a negative effect. The chance is 100% when losing a Chosen One.
When gaining Runes, a Valkyrie has a 12% chance to receive 1 extra rune per merge rank.
Valkyries of merge rank 5+ have a 20% chance to perform an extra shot when attacking, dealing 210% damage against the first monster.
If there are at least 5 Valkyries on the field, the damage they deal increases by 6% per merge rank.

When a Chosen One is lost, all Valkyries of the maximum merge rank call down a heavenly strike against the first monster, dealing 30% of the Valkyrie's guaranteed critical damage in the area. The damage depends on the unit's merge rank. After 150 runes, all Valkyries have their chance of dealing critical damage increased by 5%.

The Vampire can now bite any units of the same merge rank or lower. When bitten, the target unit becomes empowered for 15s, increasing its damage to monsters bitten by 15% for every rank of the Vampire that bit it. After 2 biting, the Vampire cannot bite units for 20s; with rank increase, the time decreases by 1s.

The Vampire can now bite any units of the same merge rank or lower. When bitten, the target unit loses a merge rank, and the Vampire becomes empowered for 20s. When the unit starts attacking the first target, the unit's damage and attack speed increase by 300%, and it also deals 100% area damage.

When biting a unit, the Vampire releases a flock of bats that fly over all monsters, dealing damage equal to 1% of the monster's current health and draining mana equal to 10% of the cost of the bitten monsters.

When biting a unit, the Vampire sends a bat to an opponent's unit, infecting it with vampirism for 6s. The player dealing the effect steals 30% mana from each monster killed by an infected unit. When the effect expires, there is a 20% chance of transmitting the effect to a random unit.
The Vampire's damage becomes poison damage. All bitten monsters take poison damage increased by 35%.
The armor of bitten monsters decreases by 10% and by another 10% for each empowered unit, but cannot be decreased by more than 30%.

Once every 20s, a random Vampire will become a Lord. When a unit is bitten, the Lord raises a big Wight in front of the opponent's first monster. The Wight speeds up neighboring monsters by 20%. Once the Wight is summoned, the Vampire loses the Lord status. In Co-Op mode, the Wight appears at the start of the path and slows down the monsters.
The Witch releases 1 additional curse/charm. The additional curse will disappear 4 seconds after impact.

The Witch stops cursing/enchanting units. Instead, every merge buffs nearby units for 4% of the damage increase effect. The strength of the effect is increased by 25% for each merge rank of the Witch. The bonus stacks up to 30 times.
A Witch with 3 merge rank and above will transform into a random unit every 25 seconds, activating her merge effect.
Witches of merge rank 3 and higher will attack 1 additional target and receive a 2% chance of instantly killing common monsters.

At the start of a battle, the Witch will create a magic circle tile on the player's field. Merging units within the circle will grant 100 mana and 40% of the summoning cost, and then the circle will be transferred. The circle is also randomly transferred at the end of each wave.

At the start of the battle, the Witch creates a cursed circle on one tile of the opponent's field in PvP or the ally's field in Co-Op. Units located within the circle receive 60% healing effect in PvP and 60% damage increase effect in Co-Op and single player modes.

The witches start to heal monsters on the opponent's path when merging in PvP, or inflict damage as a percentage of current health in Co-Op and single player modes. Healing and damage are equal to 10% and increase with merge rank by 30% of the base value.

Increases the maximum number of clones in a cyclone to 6 and summons them when a clone appears on the field. Upon attaining 30 Feat Charges, clone and cyclone damage is increased by 40%. Tapping a clone of merge rank 2+ will destroy it and summon more clones of merge rank 1. Their number is equal to the clone's merge rank minus 1.

Original Wukong's damage is increased by 200%, and he begins spinning his staff and attacking only in an area around himself. Clones no longer create cyclones, but the genuine Wukong can swap places with clones of any rank. A clone has a 15% chance to spawn with a banana; moving Wukong to such a clone will grant Feat Charges.
Merging clones has a 100% chance of purifying Wukong or a clone. Priority is given to the genuine one.
Now, every 7 Feat Charges grants a merge rank to the genuine Wukong.
Cyclones and Wukong in the Wind Dance reduce the target's armor by 0.5% per hit (maximum 30%).
The damage dealt by Wukong and clones is increased by 8% for each clone on the field (maximum 40%).
Wukongs have their critical strike chance increased by 5%. Cyclones and Wukong in the Wind Dance deal 5% more damage.

When the Zealot receives mana on a neighboring tile he has a 30% chance of summoning a force of light, dealing area damage equal to its attack and 30% of its current mana (maximum 10000). Force of light slows monsters by 25%.

When the Zealot receives mana on a neighboring tile he has a 30% chance of summoning a force of light, dealing area damage equal to its attack and 30% of its current mana (maximum 10000). Force of light increases mana from monsters by 10%.
Increases damage when there is 3000 mana available by 20%.
Increases damage when there is 10000 mana available by 30%.
When the Zealot receives mana on a neighboring tile he has a 35% probability of increasing his attack speed by 40% for 5 seconds.

When the Zealot receives mana on a neighboring tile he has a 35% probability of receiving a relic of righteousness, which increases his damage by 3 point(s) for each relic. The number of relics must not be more than 100.

When surrounded by other Zealots diagonally, takes the form of an Archon. In this form his attacks summon a force of light with a 4% chance. Getting mana in a neighbouring tile has a 35% chance to increase the Zealots' critical chance for 4% (+50% per merge rank), lasting 4 seconds.
